name: Finish Him
criteria: Somebody does any sort of Mortal Kombat, finishing move at the end of a point.

name: Paddles and Pals
criteria: Make a Korean heart with your fingers after a good play, kinda looks like Steph Curry Curry money

name: Nasty Nelson
criteria: The server hits the opposing team member at the net to score a point

name: Spike!
criteria: The player spikes the ball so fast, the opposing team didn't have time to react, winning the point

name: Out of Bounds
description: That ball had a flight reservation
criteria: Player hits the ball far out of bounds with excessive force

name: Net Magnet
criteria: Player hits a shot into the net.

name: The Statue
criteria: Player stands completely still while the ball lands near them without attempting to play it

name: Friendly Fire
criteria: Player hits the ball directly at their own partner or interferes with their partner's shot

name: Kitchen Violation
criteria: Player volleys the ball while standing in the non-volley zone

name: Wrong Way
criteria: Player moves in the wrong direction to get to the ball

name: You Thought That Was Out?
criteria: Player moves aside or lets the ball pass thinking it was going out, but it lands in bounds

name: Close Enough
criteria: A shot that was very close to being in or very close to being a great shot, but just barely missed

name: Lucky Bounce
description: Sometimes the ball just goes your way
criteria: A shot that clips the net or takes an unexpected bounce and still lands in

name: Baby Steps
criteria: Player makes a basic but correct shot or play for their skill level

name: At Least You Showed Up
criteria: Player is visibly present on the court but makes minimal impactful plays during a rally

name: Nice Try
description: You gave it a shot, just... not a great one
criteria: Player attempts a shot that clearly misses or goes way out of bounds but showed effort

name: Whiff
criteria: Player attempts a simple shot but completely misses the ball, resulting in an unforced error

name: Platinum Level Up
description: Ranked up within the Platinum tier
criteria: Awarded when a player ranks up to a Platinum division

name: Gold Level Up
description: Ranked up within the Gold tier
criteria: Awarded when a player ranks up to a Gold division

name: Silver Level Up
description: Ranked up within the Silver tier
criteria: Awarded when a player ranks up to a Silver division

name: Bronze Level Up
description: Ranked up within the Bronze tier
criteria: Awarded when a player ranks up to a Bronze or Iron division

name: Heated Battle
criteria: Both teams trade the lead back and forth multiple times during a game, with visible intensity and competitive energy from all players

name: Crowd Pleaser
criteria: A point so entertaining that multiple players visibly react with laughing, clapping, hands on head, or other amazed reactions

name: Good Game
criteria: Players from both teams shake hands, fist bump, or show mutual respect after a point or at the end of a game

name: Epic Rally
criteria: Both sides sustain a rally of 15 or more consecutive shots back and forth

name: Chest Bump
criteria: Players chest bump their teammate as a celebration

name: Point to the Sky
criteria: Player points to the sky after scoring or making a great play

name: Flex
criteria: Player strikes a flex or muscle pose after a big play

name: Moonwalk
criteria: Player does a moonwalk or backwards slide on the court as a celebration

name: Air Guitar
criteria: Player plays air guitar as a celebration after scoring or winning a point

name: Bow to the Crowd
description: Take a bow, you earned it
criteria: Player takes a bow toward the camera or audience after a good play

name: High Five Combo
criteria: Players do an elaborate multi-step high five or handshake celebration with their partner

name: Dance Break
description: Bust a move on the court
criteria: Player busts out a quick dance move between points or after scoring

name: Fist Pump
criteria: Player does an enthusiastic fist pump celebration after a good play

name: Victory Spin
criteria: Player jumps and does a 360 degree spin as a celebration

name: Airborne
criteria: Player hits the ball while both feet are off the ground, jumping to make the shot

name: Paddle Crown
criteria: Player holds their paddle on top of their head like a crown after winning a point

name: Fake Out
criteria: Player fakes a hard shot with a big wind-up motion but softly dinks the ball over the net instead, catching the opponent off guard

name: Paddle Tap Celebration
criteria: Players tap paddles together with their partner after winning a point

name: One-Handed Backhand
criteria: Player hits a clean one-handed backhand that wins the point

name: Spin Doctor
criteria: Player spins their paddle in their hand between points in a showy fashion

name: Tweener
criteria: Player hits a shot between their legs

name: No Look Dink
criteria: Player hits a dink while visibly looking away from the ball

name: Behind the Back
description: Hit a sneaky behind the back shot
criteria: Player hits a shot from behind their back or behind their head

name: Paddle Flip
description: Show off with a paddle toss
criteria: Player tosses their paddle in the air and catches it cleanly

name: Challenge Champion
description: Completed a full set of challenge badges
criteria: Complete all 3 challenge badges in a single set

name: Legendary Look
criteria: Awarded by the app when a player unlocks a gold avatar drop from earning a badge

name: Fresh Fit
criteria: Awarded by the app when a player unlocks a silver avatar drop from earning a badge

name: New Look
criteria: Awarded by the app when a player unlocks a bronze avatar drop from earning a badge

name: One of a Kind
description: Earned a legendary badge (top 1% of players)
criteria: Awarded by the app when a player earns a badge that fewer than 1% of players have

name: Exclusive Club
description: Earned a very rare badge (top 3% of players)
criteria: Awarded by the app when a player earns a badge that fewer than 3% of players have

name: Rare Find
description: Earned a rare badge (top 10% of players)
criteria: Awarded by the app when a player earns a badge that fewer than 10% of players have

name: Early Bird
criteria: Awarded by the app when a user plays a session that starts before 7 AM local time

name: Night Owl
criteria: Awarded by the app when a user plays a session that starts after 10 PM local time

name: Social Butterfly
criteria: Awarded by the app when a user has played matches against 5 unique opponents

name: Trophy Case
criteria: Awarded by the app when a user has earned 25 different badge types

name: Badge Collector
criteria: Awarded by the app when a user has earned 10 different badge types

name: Iron Streak
criteria: Awarded by the app when a user plays sessions on 7 consecutive days

name: On Fire
criteria: Awarded by the app when a user plays sessions on 3 consecutive days

name: Weekend Warrior
criteria: Awarded by the app when a user plays at least one session on 4 consecutive weekends

name: Veteran
criteria: Awarded by the app when a user has completed 50 sessions

name: Regular
criteria: Awarded by the app when a user has completed 10 sessions

name: Thousand Club
criteria: Awarded by the app when a user has created 1,000 total highlights

name: Century Club
criteria: Awarded by the app when a user has created 100 total highlights

name: Button Masher
description: Created your first highlight with a bluetooth button
criteria: Awarded by the app when a user creates their first highlight via a bluetooth button click

name: Clip Creator
description: Created your first highlight through the app
criteria: Awarded by the app when a user creates their first highlight via the app interface

name: First Share
description: Shared highlights for the first time
criteria: Awarded by the app when a user shares a highlight, highlight group, or all match highlights for the first time

name: Top 10
criteria: Awarded by the app when a user's XP ranking places them in the Top 10

name: Game On
criteria: Awarded by the app when a user completes their very first session

name: First Session
criteria: Awarded by the app when a user starts their very first session

name: Lob Recovery Duo
criteria: After being lobbed, both partners rotate back and recover the point as a coordinated unit

name: Communication Kings
criteria: Partners visibly communicate on a call (mine, yours, switch) that leads to a clean play

name: Pinch and Punch
criteria: Partners both move toward the middle to take away the center of the court and force a wide error

name: Double Reset
criteria: Both partners each reset a hard-driven ball during the same rally, neutralizing sustained pressure

name: Serve and Setup
criteria: Player hits a strategic serve that sets up their partner to put away the next ball

name: Coverage Duo
criteria: Both partners move in sync to cover lobs, dinks, and drives without leaving gaps on the court

name: Tag Team Takedown
criteria: Partners work together over multiple shots to systematically break down the opponents and win the point

name: Two Up Pressure
criteria: Both partners advance to the kitchen line together and win the point with coordinated net play

name: Switch Play
criteria: Partners smoothly switch sides mid-rally to cover the court and maintain optimal positioning

name: Wall of Hands
criteria: Both partners defend a flurry of speed ups together at the kitchen line, resetting or winning the point as a unit

name: Body Bagger
criteria: Player speeds up directly at the opponent's body, jamming them and winning the point

name: Serve Returner
criteria: Player hits a deep return of serve that pushes the serving team back and prevents an easy third shot

name: Scorpion Tail
description: Behind the back or trick shot
criteria: Player hits a behind the back, between the legs, or other unorthodox trick shot that keeps the rally alive or wins the point

name: Transition Zone Warrior
criteria: Player hits a winning shot or effective reset while stuck in the transition zone between baseline and kitchen

name: Poach King
criteria: Player crosses into their partner's side to intercept and put away a volley

name: Shake and Bake
criteria: Player drives a third shot hard while their partner crashes the net and puts away the volley

name: Drop Volley Touch
criteria: Player hits a soft drop volley off a hard shot that dies in the kitchen

name: Backhand Assassin
criteria: Player hits a backhand shot that wins the point outright with power or placement

name: Firefight Victor
criteria: Player wins a fast hands battle at the kitchen line with quick reflexes and shot selection

name: Highlight Reel
criteria: Player makes an exceptional, crowd-pleasing play that would be worthy of a highlight reel

name: Momentum Shift
description: Changed the energy of the match
criteria: Player makes a play that visibly shifts the momentum or energy of the match

name: Smart Play
criteria: Player uses deception, misdirection, or tactical intelligence to win a point

name: Steady Eddie
criteria: Player goes through the entire clip without making an unforced error

name: Hustler
criteria: Player visibly gives full effort chasing down a difficult ball, even if they don't win the point

name: Quick Reflexes
criteria: Player reacts to a fast shot with an impressive reflex save or return

name: Clutch Performer
criteria: Player executes an excellent shot or play during a high-pressure moment or turning point

name: Iron Wall
criteria: Player makes 3 or more consecutive defensive saves or retrieves that keep them in the point

name: Comeback Kid
criteria: A player or team was losing and came back to win the point or shift momentum significantly

name: Rally Master
criteria: Players sustain a rally of 8 or more consecutive shots without an error

name: Lob Genius
criteria: Player hits a lob over the opponent's head that lands in and cannot be retrieved

name: Serve Ace
criteria: Player hits a serve that the opponent cannot return at all

name: Reset Wizard
criteria: Player successfully resets a hard-driven ball back into a soft dink, neutralizing the opponent's attack

name: Stacking Pro
criteria: Team executes a stacking play correctly, switching sides efficiently to keep their preferred positioning

name: ATP Shot
criteria: Player hits the ball around the net post (ATP) successfully

name: Speed Up Specialist
criteria: Player successfully speeds up a dink rally with a well-timed attack that wins the point or creates an advantage

name: Erne Machine
criteria: Player jumps around or over the kitchen to hit a volley (an erne)

name: Third Shot Drop Artist
criteria: Player executes a third shot drop that lands softly in the kitchen and allows their team to advance to the net

name: Dink Master
criteria: Player hits a dink that is perfectly placed, low over the net, and forces the opponent into a difficult position

name: Kitchen King
criteria: Player executes 3 or more quality dinks at the kitchen line in a single rally

name: Seam Finder
criteria: Hitter finds and exploits the gap between two blockers to get a kill

name: Pipe Hitter
criteria: Player attacks from the back row (pipe) and gets a kill

name: Dig Machine
criteria: Player makes 2 or more impressive digs in the same rally or sequence

name: Tip and Roll
criteria: Player uses a soft tip or roll shot over the block that drops for a point

name: Jousting Champion
criteria: Player wins a contested ball at the top of the net against an opponent

name: Perfect Set
criteria: Setter delivers a perfectly placed set that gives the hitter an ideal attacking opportunity

name: Service Ace
criteria: Player serves an ace that hits the floor untouched or is shanked unplayable

name: Pancake Save
criteria: Player dives and gets their hand flat under the ball (pancake) to keep the rally alive

name: Roof Block
criteria: Player blocks a spike straight down on the attacker's side for a point

name: Hammer
criteria: Player hits a spike that hits the floor untouched or is impossible to dig

name: Four Corners
criteria: Player systematically moves the opponent to all four corners of the court within a single rally

name: Volley Killer
criteria: Player takes the ball early with a volley that cuts off the opponent's time and wins the point

name: Deception King
criteria: Player prepares for one shot but plays another, completely deceiving the opponent

name: Lob Lifeline
criteria: Player retrieves a difficult shot and lobs it high to the back corner, buying time to recover

name: Ghost Move
criteria: Player demonstrates excellent ghosting movement, gliding to the ball and recovering smoothly to the T

name: Drop Dead
criteria: Player hits a drop shot that dies near the front wall tin with minimal bounce

name: Boast Master
criteria: Player hits a boast shot off the side wall to the front that wrong-foots the opponent

name: Rail Runner
criteria: Player hits a drive that hugs the side wall tightly, making it very difficult to return

name: T Dominator
criteria: Player consistently recovers to the T position and controls the center of the court throughout the rally

name: Nick Finder
criteria: Player hits a shot that catches the nick (junction of wall and floor) and rolls out unreturnable

name: Back Wall Save
criteria: Player reads a ball off the back wall and makes a successful return from a defensive position

name: Drive Serve Bomb
criteria: Player hits a low hard drive serve that jams the opponent or results in a weak return

name: Around the World
criteria: Player hits an around-the-wall shot that uses all three walls and lands deep in the back court

name: Pass Shot Pro
criteria: Player hits a pass shot down the line that goes past the opponent cleanly

name: Center Court Boss
criteria: Player consistently maintains center court position and forces opponent to hit from behind

name: Splat Shot
criteria: Player hits a splat shot into the side wall at a sharp angle that dies quickly

name: Pinch Perfect
criteria: Player hits a pinch shot into the side wall-front wall combination that dies in the front court

name: Z-Serve Ace
criteria: Player hits a z-serve that the opponent cannot return cleanly

name: Ceiling Master
criteria: Player uses a ceiling shot that drives the opponent deep into the back court

name: Kill Shot King
criteria: Player hits a kill shot that rolls out low off the front wall and is unretrievable

name: Speed Demon
criteria: Player wins a rapid-fire close-to-the-table exchange with quick hands and reflexes

name: Placement Pro
criteria: Player wins a point by placing the ball precisely to the opponent's weak spot or wide angle

name: Net Roller
criteria: Ball hits the net and rolls over for an unreturnable winner

name: Smash Finish
criteria: Player finishes a point with a decisive smash that the opponent cannot retrieve

name: Edge Ball Luck
description: Won a point off the edge
criteria: Player wins a point when the ball clips the edge or net cord

name: Chop Block
criteria: Player uses a chop or block to completely change the pace and win the point

name: Counter Attack
criteria: Player wins a high-speed counter-loop exchange with a well-placed finishing shot

name: Backhand Flick
criteria: Player attacks a short ball with an aggressive backhand flick over the net

name: Spin Doctor
criteria: Player serves with heavy or deceptive spin that causes the opponent to misread and miss or pop up the return

name: Loop Lightning
criteria: Player hits a forehand loop with heavy topspin that is unreturnable

name: Team Sync
criteria: Both partners move laterally in sync, covering the court as a unit during a rally

name: Smash Finish
criteria: Player hits an overhead smash through the court that bounces over the back glass for a winner

name: Double Glass
criteria: Player successfully returns a ball that bounced off two walls (side and back or vice versa)

name: Lob Perfecto
criteria: Player hits a lob from a defensive position that pushes both opponents back from the net

name: Net Dominator
criteria: Both partners maintain strong net position together for an extended rally, winning volleys consistently

name: Bajada Bomb
criteria: Player hits an aggressive bajada off the back glass that wins the point outright

name: Chiquita Touch
criteria: Player hits a soft chiquita at the feet of the net player that forces a difficult low volley

name: Wall Wizard
criteria: Player reads and plays a ball off the back or side wall with excellent timing and placement

name: Vibora Venom
criteria: Player hits a vibora with heavy spin that bounces awkwardly off the glass and is unreturnable

name: Bandeja Boss
criteria: Player executes a textbook bandeja with proper slice and placement that maintains net position

name: Gutter Recovery
criteria: Bowler throws a gutter ball but follows it up immediately with a strike or spare

name: Clean Frame
description: Strike or spare in every frame
criteria: Bowler does not leave an open frame in the visible portion of the clip

name: Power Player
criteria: Bowler throws a shot with visibly high revolution rate and strong backend hook

name: Spare Machine
criteria: Bowler converts 3 or more spare attempts in a row

name: Lane Adjuster
criteria: Bowler makes a visible adjustment in positioning or angle that results in an improved shot

name: Pocket Finder
criteria: Bowler hits the pocket cleanly on 3 or more consecutive shots

name: Perfect Release
criteria: Bowler demonstrates a clean release with good rotation and consistent hand position

name: Split Converter
criteria: Bowler successfully picks up a split spare

name: Brooklyn Strike
criteria: Bowler gets a strike hitting the opposite side of the head pin (brooklyn)

name: Turkey
criteria: Bowler throws three consecutive strikes

name: Pick and Roll Pro
criteria: Player executes a pick and roll play that leads to a score, either as the screener or ball handler

name: Shot Swatter
criteria: Player emphatically blocks a shot attempt, especially at the rim

name: Fast Break Finish
criteria: Player finishes a fast break opportunity with a layup, dunk, or assist in transition

name: And One
criteria: Player finishes a basket while being fouled, showing toughness and skill

name: Glass Cleaner
criteria: Player grabs a contested rebound with authority, especially an offensive rebound

name: Dime Dropper
criteria: Player delivers a creative or perfectly placed pass that leads directly to an easy score

name: Lockdown D
criteria: Player forces a turnover, blocks a shot, or completely shuts down an offensive possession with great defense

name: Posterized
criteria: Player dunks the ball over or through a defender who is attempting to contest

name: Ankle Breaker
criteria: Player executes a crossover or dribble move that causes the defender to stumble or freeze

name: Splash
criteria: Player hits a three-pointer from well beyond the arc

name: Defense to Offense
criteria: Player retrieves a smash or attack and immediately transitions to an offensive position

name: Flick Serve Surprise
criteria: Player catches the opponent off guard with a flick serve that results in a weak return

name: Rotation Ace
criteria: In doubles, the team executes a smooth front/back to side/side rotation or vice versa

name: Drive Master
criteria: Player wins a fast flat drive exchange with a well-placed finishing drive

name: Cross Court Killer
criteria: Player hits a sharp cross court shot that wrong-foots the opponent

name: Lift and Shift
criteria: Player uses well-placed lifts to move the opponent around the court and create openings

name: Clear Winner
criteria: Player hits a fast attacking clear that pushes the opponent behind the baseline

name: Deceptive Drop
criteria: Player disguises a drop shot with a full swing motion, catching the opponent off guard

name: Net Assassin
criteria: Player plays a tight net shot that tumbles over and dies on the opponent's side

name: Smash King
criteria: Player hits a smash that is unreturnable or forces a weak lift

name: Court Coverage King
criteria: Player covers the full width of the court to retrieve a shot that seemed out of reach

name: Slice Master
criteria: Player uses a slice shot that stays very low and forces the opponent into an awkward position

name: Moonball Buster
criteria: Player aggressively attacks a high bouncing ball with a put-away shot

name: Break Point Clutch
criteria: Player wins a critical break point either on serve or return

name: Winner Machine
criteria: Player hits an outright winner that the opponent has no chance of reaching

name: Drop Shot Wizard
criteria: Player disguises a drop shot that the opponent cannot reach in time

name: Passing Shot Artist
criteria: Player hits a passing shot past an opponent who is at the net

name: Net Rusher
criteria: Player approaches the net and finishes the point with a clean volley or overhead

name: Baseline Brawler
criteria: Player wins a point with 4 or more powerful groundstrokes from the baseline

name: Ace
criteria: Player hits a serve that the opponent cannot touch

name: Special Teams Ace
criteria: Player makes an impactful play on special teams such as a big return, perfect punt, or kickoff coverage tackle

name: Escape Artist
criteria: Quarterback escapes pressure in the pocket and extends the play to make a throw or gain yards

name: No Fly Zone
criteria: Defensive back breaks up a pass or completely blankets a receiver on a route

name: Route Runner
criteria: Receiver runs a sharp route that creates clear separation from the defender

name: Pancake Block
criteria: Offensive lineman puts a defender on the ground with a decisive block

name: Sack Master
criteria: Defensive player sacks the quarterback behind the line of scrimmage

name: Pick Six
criteria: Defensive player intercepts a pass and returns it for a touchdown or a significant gain

name: Truck Stick
criteria: Ball carrier runs through or over a defender who is attempting to tackle them

name: Toe Drag Swag
description: Sideline catch with feet in bounds
criteria: Receiver makes a catch near the sideline while keeping both feet in bounds

name: Bomb Squad
criteria: Quarterback completes a pass of 30 or more yards downfield

name: Breakaway Beauty
criteria: Player scores on a breakaway with a clean finish past the goalie

name: Faceoff King
criteria: Player cleanly wins a faceoff in a critical situation

name: Backcheck Hero
criteria: Forward hustles back to break up an opposing rush or prevent a scoring chance

name: Hip Check
criteria: Player delivers a clean, hard body check that separates the opponent from the puck

name: Tape to Tape
criteria: Player delivers a crisp pass through traffic that lands perfectly on a teammate's stick

name: Brick Wall
criteria: Goalie makes a spectacular save that robs a clear scoring chance

name: One-Timer Bomb
criteria: Player receives a pass and fires a one-timer without stopping the puck

name: Dangle King
criteria: Player dekes past a defender or goalie with impressive stick handling

name: Sniper
criteria: Player scores with a accurate wrist shot that picks a corner

name: Top Shelf
description: Scored high glove or blocker side
criteria: Player shoots and scores by placing the puck in the top corner of the net

name: Drop and Stop
criteria: Player disguises a drop shot that dies near the net with the foam ball barely bouncing

name: Stakes Clutch
criteria: Player wins a point during a high-pressure stakes moment that shifts the scoring momentum

name: Full Rip
criteria: Player loads up and rips a full tennis-style swing that produces an unreturnable winner

name: Kick Serve King
criteria: Player hits an underhand serve with heavy topspin that kicks up high and pushes the opponent back

name: Knuckleball Serve
criteria: Player delivers an underhand serve with knuckleball movement that causes the opponent to misread and mis-hit the return

name: Volley Crasher
criteria: Player charges the net and puts away a volley for a winner, taking advantage of no kitchen rule

name: Baseline Bomber
criteria: Player hits a full-swing groundstroke from the baseline that wins the point outright

name: Net Rebound Hero
description: Won a point off a net rebound
criteria: Player saves a net rebound and the rally continues with their team ultimately winning the point

name: Body Save
description: Kept the ball alive with a body part
criteria: Player uses a hand, foot, chest, or head to save a net rebound and keep the rally alive

name: Spin Surgeon
criteria: Player rips a shot with visible heavy spin that kicks off the court and is difficult to return
